description:
OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton is central Ohio's premier seasonal outdoor ice skating destination, located in the Easton Town Center area of northeast Columbus. This family-friendly seasonal rink operates as part of the expanded OhioHealth Ice Trails system, providing the Columbus community with a magical outdoor skating experience during winter months. OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton offers public skating, figure skating sessions, and recreational programming for skaters of all ages and abilities. The outdoor skating environment creates a festive, memorable experience for Columbus families seeking winter activities, while the Easton location provides convenient access to shopping, dining, and entertainment in one of Columbus's most popular communities.

OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton serves Columbus families, casual recreational skaters, children learning to skate, and winter enthusiasts seeking outdoor skating experiences. The rink accommodates figure skaters enjoying recreational ice time, recreational skaters of all abilities, and beginners taking advantage of professional skate rentals. The Easton Town Center location makes the rink easily accessible to northeast Columbus residents, shoppers visiting Easton, and day-trippers seeking winter activities. Public skating sessions welcome all skill levels, making ice skating accessible to families regardless of prior experience. The outdoor setting creates a festive atmosphere that enhances the skating experience beyond what indoor facilities can offer.

What distinguishes OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton is its outdoor seasonal charm, family-friendly focus, and integration with the vibrant Easton Town Center community. The rink provides Columbus families with a premium winter skating destination featuring professional amenities, skate rentals, and convenient location near shopping and dining. Seasonal operation from approximately November through February creates a limited-availability destination that draws eager skaters during Ohio's winter months. The OhioHealth Ice Trails system's commitment to community ice sports makes this rink an essential Columbus winter destination. Contact OhioHealth or check the Ice Trails website for current operating dates, public skating schedules, rental information, and any special events or holiday programming.
what_to_know: Seasonal outdoor operation: typically November through February (weather dependent; confirm opening/closing dates in advance)
Located at Easton Town Center: convenient access to shopping, dining, and entertainment alongside skating
Professional skate rentals available; bring your own skates or rent quality equipment at competitive rates
Public skating sessions available during operating hours; check schedule for times and any special event bookings
Family-friendly atmosphere with outdoor seasonal charm—perfect for holiday celebrations and winter activities
Easily accessible from northeast Columbus via I-270 and surrounding surface streets with ample Easton parking
Weather-dependent operation: heavy snow, ice conditions, or extreme temperatures may cause closures; check status before visiting

faq.question: When does OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton open and close each season?
faq.answer: OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton operates seasonally, typically from November through February, weather permitting. Exact opening and closing dates vary by year and weather conditions. Contact OhioHealth directly or check their website before visiting to confirm the rink is open, as cold snaps, ice conditions, and maintenance may affect hours or cause temporary closures.

faq.question: Can I rent skates at OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton?
faq.answer: Yes, professional skate rentals are available on-site for visitors of all ages and shoe sizes. Rental rates are competitive for a premium outdoor facility. Bring your own skates if you prefer, or rent equipment from the rink if you don't have gear.

faq.question: What are the public skating hours and how crowded is the rink?
faq.answer: Public skating sessions are available during operating hours, with specific time slots posted on the OhioHealth website or at the rink. Evenings and weekends are busiest; consider visiting during afternoon weekday sessions for less crowded skating. Check the website or call ahead for the current schedule.

faq.question: Is there food and entertainment at OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton?
faq.answer: Yes, the Easton Town Center location provides access to multiple restaurants, cafes, and seasonal food vendors. You can enjoy beverages, snacks, and full meals at nearby Easton establishments. The location combines skating with shopping and dining, making a complete winter outing possible.

faq.question: How do I get to OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton and where do I park?
faq.answer: OhioHealth Ice Trails at Easton is located in the Easton Town Center area of northeast Columbus. The location is easily accessible via I-270 and connecting routes. Ample parking is available at Easton Town Center with convenient access to the rink. GPS and Google Maps provide turn-by-turn directions from any Columbus location.

description:
OhioHealth Ice Haus / Chiller Dublin is one of Ohio's premier ice skating facilities, located at 3600 Bright Road in Dublin, Ohio. This dual-sheet complex is part of the renowned Chiller chain, which operates Ohio's most comprehensive ice sports network serving the Columbus metropolitan area. Chiller Dublin accommodates figure skating, recreational skating, and competitive ice hockey with professional-grade ice sheets and extensive programming. The facility serves Dublin, Powell, Columbus, and the northwest Columbus suburbs with some of Ohio's strongest youth hockey development programs and figure skating coaching. Chiller Dublin's affiliation with the Columbus Blue Jackets NHL organization reflects the facility's elite status and commitment to competitive ice sports excellence.

Chiller Dublin serves competitive ice hockey players, aspiring figure skaters pursuing serious training, recreational skaters enjoying public sessions, and families enrolling children in learn-to-skate programs. The dual ice sheets enable simultaneous hosting of youth and adult hockey leagues, figure skating club operations, learn-to-skate classes, and public recreational skating. Chiller's partnership with the Columbus Blue Jackets organization brings elite hockey development resources and community connections. Figure skating coaching at Chiller Dublin attracts dedicated athletes pursuing competitive advancement. Public skating sessions accommodate recreational skaters and families. The facility's comprehensive programming serves skaters at all levels from absolute beginner through elite competitive athletes.

What distinguishes Chiller Dublin is its dual-sheet capacity, Columbus Blue Jackets affiliation, elite hockey programming, and position as central Ohio's premier ice sports destination. The facility's reputation attracts elite youth travel hockey teams and serious figure skating students from throughout Ohio and beyond. Chiller's multi-facility network allows flexible programming and specialized ice time allocation. The Dublin location provides convenient access to northwest Columbus suburbs via I-270 and surface streets. Competitive programs, professional coaching, and comprehensive facility amenities make Chiller Dublin a complete ice skating destination. Visit thechiller.com for current public skating schedules, learn-to-skate registration, competitive league information, and figure skating coaching details.
what_to_know: Dual ice sheets support multiple simultaneous programs: hockey tournaments, figure skating, lessons, and public recreational skating
Columbus Blue Jackets affiliation: elite youth hockey development programs with NHL connections
Public skating sessions available; check thechiller.com for current schedules and times
Learn-to-skate programs available for children and beginners with certified instructors and progressive skill building
Youth and adult hockey leagues featuring competitive programming and elite player development
Figure skating coaching available for dedicated skaters pursuing competitive training and development
Located in Dublin/Powell area: convenient access via I-270 to northwest Columbus suburbs

faq.question: What are the public skating hours at Chiller Dublin?
faq.answer: Chiller Dublin operates public skating sessions throughout the week, with schedules varying by day and subject to change for tournaments, leagues, and special events. Visit thechiller.com to view current public skating times, or call the facility directly. Peak times are typically evenings and weekends; consider visiting during afternoon weekday sessions for less crowded skating.

faq.question: Does Chiller Dublin offer learn-to-skate programs for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, Chiller Dublin offers comprehensive learn-to-skate programs for children and adults of all ages and abilities. Classes progress through skill levels from absolute beginner to advanced recreational skating. Private lessons are also available. Register through thechiller.com or contact the facility for current program schedules, pricing, and registration information.

faq.question: What hockey programs and leagues are available at Chiller Dublin?
faq.answer: Chiller Dublin hosts elite youth hockey programs, youth competitive leagues, and adult recreational and competitive hockey leagues. The facility's Columbus Blue Jackets affiliation brings professional-level player development resources. Contact Chiller Dublin directly for information about league registration, age groups, competitive divisions, and season schedules.

faq.question: Does Chiller Dublin have figure skating coaching and competitive programs?
faq.answer: Yes, Chiller Dublin hosts figure skating coaching and club operations serving skaters pursuing competitive training and development. The facility provides professional coaching in singles skating and other disciplines. Contact Chiller Dublin or visit thechiller.com for information about figure skating programs, coaching availability, lesson scheduling, and fees.

faq.question: Where is Chiller Dublin located and what's the best way to get there?
faq.answer: Chiller Dublin is located at 3600 Bright Road in Dublin, Ohio, in the northwest Columbus area. The facility is easily accessible via I-270 and connecting routes. The Dublin location serves Powell, Westerville, Columbus, and surrounding northwest suburbs. GPS and Google Maps provide directions, and ample parking is available at the facility.

description:
CSU Ice Arena is downtown Cleveland's premier urban ice skating facility, located at 2000 Prospect Avenue East in Cleveland's university district. This single-sheet facility is operated by Cleveland State University and serves as Cleveland State's athletic venue while also providing public skating opportunities for the broader downtown Cleveland and greater Cleveland community. CSU Ice Arena accommodates figure skating, recreational skating, and competitive ice hockey, serving university students, faculty, families, and skating enthusiasts throughout the Cleveland region. The facility's downtown location makes it uniquely accessible to urban Cleveland residents and workers, providing rare year-round ice access in Cleveland's core. CSU Ice Arena has established itself as a cultural institution supporting ice sports in downtown Cleveland and serving as the primary skating resource for the Cleveland State community.

CSU Ice Arena serves Cleveland State University students, faculty, and staff seeking recreational activities and athletic training, downtown Cleveland families with children learning to skate, competitive figure skaters pursuing training opportunities, recreational hockey players enjoying league play, and the broader Cleveland community supporting winter sports culture. The university affiliation creates a college-town skating culture combining serious athletic training with casual recreational use. Cleveland State students and employees enjoy potentially reduced rates and convenient campus access. Public skating accommodates families, recreational skaters, and visitors seeking casual ice time in downtown Cleveland. Figure skating and hockey programming serve both competitive athletes and recreational participants. The downtown location makes CSU Ice Arena an essential urban skating resource for northeastern Ohio.

What distinguishes CSU Ice Arena is its unique downtown Cleveland location, Cleveland State University integration, and role as the primary public skating resource for central Cleveland. The Prospect Avenue location near the university campus makes it convenient for students, faculty, and downtown residents. The facility contributes to Cleveland's urban winter sports culture and provides essential recreational infrastructure for a region with limited indoor skating alternatives. Public skating opportunities serve the broader Cleveland community beyond the university. Contact CSU Ice Arena directly for public skating schedules, learn-to-skate program registration, hockey league details, and figure skating coaching opportunities.
what_to_know: Downtown Cleveland location: convenient access for urban residents and Cleveland State University community
Year-round operation provides consistent ice skating access for Cleveland residents and university community
Public skating sessions available; contact the facility for current schedules and times
Cleveland State students may have reduced rates or special access; inquire about university-affiliated pricing
Learn-to-skate programs available for children and beginners with professional instruction
Youth and adult hockey leagues serve the Cleveland region with competitive programming options
Parking available: check with CSU or use nearby university parking resources for venue access

faq.question: What are the public skating hours at CSU Ice Arena?
faq.answer: CSU Ice Arena operates public skating sessions throughout the week, with schedules varying by day and subject to change for Cleveland State athletic events, competitions, and special programming. Call the facility directly for current public skating times, or check if Cleveland State maintains schedule information online. College calendar events may affect availability during exam periods and holidays.

faq.question: Can Cleveland State students use CSU Ice Arena?
faq.answer: Yes, CSU Ice Arena serves the Cleveland State University community. Students have access to public skating sessions and may receive special student rates or membership benefits. Contact Cleveland State's athletic department or the arena directly for student-specific information, pricing, and any university-affiliated programs.

faq.question: Does CSU Ice Arena offer learn-to-skate programs for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, CSU Ice Arena offers learn-to-skate programs for children and adults of all ages and abilities. Classes progress through skill levels from beginner to advanced recreational skating. faq.question: Where is CSU Ice Arena located and how do I get there?
faq.answer: CSU Ice Arena is located at 2000 Prospect Avenue East in downtown Cleveland, directly adjacent to the Cleveland State University campus. The downtown location makes it easily accessible for Cleveland residents, university community members, and downtown workers. Campus parking and nearby metered lots serve visitors. GPS and Google Maps provide directions from anywhere in Cleveland.

description:
Strongsville Recreation Center Ice Arena is southwest Cleveland's family-friendly community ice skating facility, located at 18100 Royalton Road in Strongsville, Ohio. This single-sheet rink is operated by the Strongsville Parks and Recreation Department, providing residents of Strongsville, North Royalton, Berea, and surrounding southwest Cuyahoga County communities with affordable, accessible year-round ice skating. The facility accommodates figure skating, recreational skating, and competitive ice hockey, serving families seeking quality ice skating experiences at community rates. Strongsville Recreation Center functions as an essential recreational resource for southwest Cleveland suburbs, providing reliable ice access without premium pricing typical of private facilities. The rink has established itself as a trusted community institution serving multiple generations of families.

Strongsville Recreation Center serves southwest suburban families with children, recreational skaters seeking affordable ice time, young athletes developing hockey and figure skating skills, and recreational hockey players enjoying league play. The city-operated model ensures affordable pricing accessible to families regardless of income level. Public skating sessions welcome all skill levels and ages, making ice skating available to the broader community. Youth hockey leagues develop young players with recreational focus rather than elite travel team structure. Learn-to-skate programs support skill development and confidence building for beginners. Figure skating programming accommodates those pursuing lessons and recreational training. The facility's emphasis on community recreation makes it an essential resource for southwest Cuyahoga County families.

What makes Strongsville Recreation Center special is its community-operated model, affordable pricing, and emphasis on recreational family ice skating rather than premium competitive programming. The facility provides reliable ice skating access to southwest Cuyahoga County residents without the premium costs associated with private facilities. The recreational approach serves families seeking casual ice time, beginner skaters, and young players developing basic hockey skills. Strong community support and city operation ensure sustainable programming focused on resident needs. Contact Strongsville Parks and Recreation or the facility directly for public skating schedules, learn-to-skate program registration, youth hockey information, and pricing details.

faq.answer: Yes, professional skate rentals are available on-site for visitors of all ages and shoe sizes. Rental rates are competitive for a premium outdoor facility. Bring your own skates if you prefer, or rent equipment from the rink if you don't have gear.

faq.question: What are the public skating hours and is the rink crowded?
faq.answer: Public skating sessions are available during operating hours, with specific time slots posted on the OhioHealth website or at the rink. Evenings and weekends are typically busiest; consider visiting during afternoon weekday sessions for less crowded skating. Check the website or call ahead for the current schedule.

faq.answer: OhioHealth Ice Trails at Polaris is located in the Polaris area of north Columbus.
